concept_name: Electric Dipole in a Uniform External Field (τ = p × E)
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics Ch.1 §1.12 (Dipole in a Uniform External Field) — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. Sibling of torque_on_current_loop_in_field: an electric dipole of moment p in a uniform field E feels zero net force but a non-zero torque τ = p × E that rotates it toward alignment, with potential energy U = −p·E. Board/NCERT depth — torque, the force couple, stable/unstable equilibrium, and potential energy; the JEE small-oscillation period T = 2π√(I/pE) is intentionally out of scope (founder decision 2026-06-23). The four student errors targeted: (a) believing a uniform field pushes the dipole bodily along the field (net force is zero — only torque), (b) believing the dipole feels no force at all (each charge feels qE; they form a couple), (c) believing torque is maximum when p ∥ E (it is zero there; maximum at 90°), (d) believing θ = 180° is just as stable as θ = 0° (it is the energy maximum — unstable). Reuses the archetype-C rotational-dynamics renderer primitives established by the torque-loop diamond (moment vector, couple forces, τ along the rotation axis, oscillation, θ-sweep). Conforms to the EPIC-L-first directive (no epic_c_branches) and the conceptual-only directive (no mode_overrides). LIVE-INSTRUMENT CONVERSION (2026-06-30): every state is interactive (show_sliders), auto-demo motion runs until the teacher grabs a control. RULE-31 REBUILD (2026-07-05, founder-directed): 8 straightforward ~20s states — the couple and the ΣF=0 aha split into their own beats, a dedicated θ=180° unstable-flip state (damped_pendulum: park → slow creep → race through 90° → overshoot → settle at 0°), and a physics-release explore (releasing the θ slider hands the rotation to the real τ = pE sinθ damped dynamics; k scales with the live p·E).

real_world_anchor.primary: Picture a compass needle. A compass needle is a tiny dipole sitting in the Earth's field. Let it go and it swings until it points along the field, then holds there. Push it aside and it springs back. That 'springs back to alignment' behaviour is exactly the torque τ = pE sin θ doing its job — and an electric dipole in an electric field behaves the very same way.
real_world_anchor.secondary: A water molecule is a permanent electric dipole — the oxygen end is slightly negative, the two hydrogen ends slightly positive, so it carries a built-in dipole moment p. Switch on an electric field and trillions of these little dipoles twist to line up with it. That mass alignment of water dipoles is the same τ = p × E you are about to watch on a single dipole, multiplied a billion-billion times.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: Inside a microwave oven, a rapidly reversing electric field grabs the dipole water molecules in your dal and flips them back and forth about 2.45 billion times every second. The friction of all that flipping is the heat that warms your food. Same torque τ = p × E on a dipole — just an E that keeps reversing direction.

E.name: Magnitude of the uniform external field

formulas.torque_vector: τ = p × E — the vector torque is the cross product of the dipole moment with the external field; it is perpendicular to both p and E.
formulas.torque_magnitude: τ = pE sin θ — scalar magnitude; θ is the angle between p and E; τ is maximum at θ = 90° and zero at θ = 0° or 180°.
formulas.dipole_moment: p = q · 2a — the dipole moment points from the −q charge to the +q charge; its magnitude is charge times separation.
formulas.potential_energy: U = −p·E = −pE cos θ — minimum (−pE) at θ = 0°, zero at θ = 90°, maximum (+pE) at θ = 180°.
formulas.net_force_zero: F_net = (+q)E + (−q)E = 0 in a UNIFORM field — the two charge-forces are equal and opposite, so the dipole rotates but does not translate.
formulas.equilibrium_stable: θ = 0° (p ∥ E) — stable equilibrium; U is a minimum, and a small nudge produces a restoring torque back toward alignment.
formulas.equilibrium_unstable: θ = 180° (p anti-parallel to E) — unstable equilibrium; U is a maximum, and any tiny nudge grows until the dipole flips around to θ = 0°.

constraints: E must be uniform across the dipole. A non-uniform field adds a net force (the gradient term F = p·∇E) and the dipole would also translate.
The dipole is rigid — the charge separation 2a is fixed during rotation.
τ = p × E is a vector perpendicular to BOTH p and E; its magnitude is pE sin θ.
θ is measured from p to E. Positive θ gives a restoring torque toward θ = 0°.
U = −p·E is defined up to a constant; the standard NCERT reference is U = 0 at θ = 90°.
Point-dipole idealisation: the separation 2a is much smaller than the scale over which the field varies.

aha_moment.statement: Net force is zero, yet the torque is not — the dipole rotates without moving.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: The two equal-and-opposite charge forces resolve to a ΣF = 0 badge, yet the τ vector appears and the dipole turns toward E. Both facts are true at once — drag θ to feel the torque rise and fall.

STATE_1.title: A dipole and its moment p = q · 2a

scene_composition.text: An electric dipole: +q and −q a small distance apart

The dipole moment p
points from −q to +q.
Magnitude: p = q · 2a.

No external field yet.
Next: drop this dipole
into a uniform field E.
A water molecule is exactly this — a built-in electric dipole.

tts_sentences.text_en: This is an electric dipole. A positive charge and an equal negative charge, a small distance apart.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह एक electric dipole है। एक positive charge और एक बराबर negative charge, थोड़ी सी दूरी पर।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది ఒక electric dipole. ఒక positive charge మరియు సమానమైన negative charge, కొంచెం దూరంలో ఉన్నాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The yellow arrow is the dipole moment p — it points from the negative to the positive charge, with size p equals q times two a.
tts_sentences.text_hi: पीला arrow dipole moment p है — यह negative charge से positive charge की तरफ point करता है, और इसका size p equals q times two a है।
tts_sentences.text_te: పసుపు రంగు arrow dipole moment p — ఇది negative charge నుండి positive charge వైపు point చేస్తుంది, దాని size p equals q times two a.

tts_sentences.text_en: There is no field yet. Next, we drop this dipole into a uniform external field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अभी कोई field नहीं है। अगले step में, हम इस dipole को एक uniform external field में डालेंगे।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇంకా field లేదు. తర్వాత, మనం ఈ dipole ని ఒక uniform external field లో వేస్తాం.

STATE_2.title: Place it in a uniform field E

scene_composition.text: A uniform field E — the same everywhere

Every blue arrow is identical:
same length, same direction.
That is what 'uniform' means.

The dipole sits at an angle θ
to the field. Watch the yellow
arc between p and E.
What will the field do to this tilted dipole?

tts_sentences.text_en: Now switch on a uniform electric field. Every blue arrow is identical in length and direction.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब एक uniform electric field को switch on करते हैं। हर नीला arrow length और direction में identical है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు ఒక uniform electric field switch on చేద్దాం. ప్రతి నీలి arrow length మరియు direction లో identical గా ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The dipole sits tilted at an angle theta to the field. The yellow arc marks that angle between the dipole moment p and the electric field E.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Dipole field के साथ theta angle पर tilted है। पीला arc उस angle को mark करता है जो dipole moment p और electric field E के बीच में है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Dipole field తో theta angle లో tilted గా ఉంది. పసుపు రంగు arc dipole moment p మరియు electric field E మధ్య ఉన్న ఆ angle ని చూపిస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Hold that angle in mind — next, watch what this uniform field does to the tilted dipole.
tts_sentences.text_hi: उस angle को ध्यान में रखो — अगले step में देखो कि यह uniform field इस tilted dipole के साथ क्या करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఆ angle ని గుర్తుంచుకోండి — తర్వాత, ఈ uniform field ఈ tilted dipole కి ఏమి చేస్తుందో చూడండి.

STATE_3.title: Each charge feels qE — the couple

scene_composition.text: Each charge feels a force qE — equal, opposite, offset

On +q: +qE along E.
On −q: −qE against E.
Equal, opposite, different
lines of action → a COUPLE.

A couple cannot push the
dipole anywhere — it can
only TWIST it.
Next: add the two forces.

tts_sentences.text_en: Look at each charge separately. The positive charge feels a force q E, pointing along the field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर charge को अलग-अलग देखो। positive charge को एक force q E महसूस होता है, जो field की direction में point करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి charge ని విడిగా చూడండి. positive charge కి ఒక force q E అనిపిస్తుంది, అది field వైపు point చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The negative charge feels a force q E pointing the opposite way, against the field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: negative charge को भी force q E महसूस होता है, लेकिन वह opposite direction में, field के against।
tts_sentences.text_te: negative charge కి కూడా force q E అనిపిస్తుంది, కానీ అది opposite వైపు, field కి వ్యతిరేకంగా.

tts_sentences.text_en: Two forces. Equal in size. Opposite in direction. But acting on different lines. That combination is called a couple.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दो forces। size में बराबर। direction में opposite। लेकिन अलग-अलग lines पर act करते हैं। इस combination को couple कहते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు forces. size లో సమానం. direction లో opposite. కానీ వేర్వేరు lines పై act చేస్తాయి. ఈ combination ని couple అంటారు.

STATE_4.title: ΣF = 0, yet τ ≠ 0 — it turns without moving

scene_composition.text: ΣF = 0, yet τ ≠ 0 — it turns without moving

Add them: ΣF = 0 → no sliding.
But the couple twists: τ (purple)
turns the dipole toward E.
Zero net force AND non-zero torque, both at once — it turns without moving off the spot.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now add the two forces together. Plus q E and minus q E cancel exactly. The net force is zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब दोनों forces को जोड़ो। plus q E और minus q E बिल्कुल cancel हो जाती हैं। net force zero हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు రెండు forces ని కలపండి. plus q E మరియు minus q E పూర్తిగా cancel అవుతాయి. net force zero అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The couple produces a torque. The purple arrow is the torque tau, and the dipole begins to rotate toward the field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: couple एक torque produce करता है। बैंगनी arrow torque tau है, और dipole field की तरफ rotate होना शुरू हो जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: couple ఒక torque produce చేస్తుంది. ఊదా రంగు arrow torque tau, మరియు dipole field వైపు rotate అవ్వడం మొదలవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Forces decide sliding. Torques decide turning. A uniform field gives the dipole only a turn.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Forces sliding decide करती हैं। Torques turning decide करते हैं। एक uniform field dipole को सिर्फ़ एक turn देती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Forces sliding decide చేస్తాయి. Torques turning decide చేస్తాయి. ఒక uniform field dipole కి కేవలం ఒక turn ఇస్తుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: A dipole placed in a field feels no force at all, because the charges are equal and opposite.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Each charge DOES feel a force qE. The two are equal and opposite, so they form a couple — zero net force, but a real twisting torque.

misconception_watch.belief: A uniform field pushes the dipole bodily along the direction of the field.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: In a UNIFORM field the net force is exactly zero — the dipole only rotates to align with E, it does not translate.

STATE_5.title: τ = pE sin θ — sweep θ; max at 90°

scene_composition.text: Sweep θ — where is the torque biggest?

τ = pE sin θ.
Zero when p lines up with E,
grows as the dipole tilts.
This is a sine curve.

Wrong guess: θ = 0° (aligned).
Answer: θ = 90°!
τ = pE sin 90° = pE.
τ = p × E. Parallel → sin 0 = 0 → τ = 0. Perpendicular → sin 90 = 1 → τ = max.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch theta sweep from zero to one eighty and back — keep your eye on the purple torque arrow.
tts_sentences.text_hi: theta को zero से one eighty तक और वापस sweep होते देखो — बैंगनी torque arrow पर नज़र रखो।
tts_sentences.text_te: theta zero నుండి one eighty వరకు మరియు తిరిగి sweep అవ్వడం చూడండి — ఊదా torque arrow పై దృష్టి పెట్టండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The torque starts at zero, grows as the dipole tilts, and peaks — tau equals p E sine theta, a sine curve.
tts_sentences.text_hi: torque zero से शुरू होता है, dipole के tilt होने पर बढ़ता है, और peak करता है — tau equals p E sine theta, एक sine curve।
tts_sentences.text_te: torque zero నుండి మొదలై, dipole tilt అయ్యే కొద్దీ పెరిగి, peak చేరుతుంది — tau equals p E sine theta, ఒక sine curve.

tts_sentences.text_en: Many students expect the maximum at zero degrees, where p lines up with E. But that is where the torque vanishes — the maximum is at ninety.
tts_sentences.text_hi: बहुत से students maximum की उम्मीद zero degrees पर करते हैं, जहाँ p, E के साथ line up होता है। लेकिन वहीं torque गायब हो जाता है — maximum ninety पर है।
tts_sentences.text_te: చాలా students maximum zero degrees వద్ద అని అనుకుంటారు, అక్కడ p, E తో line up అవుతుంది. కానీ అక్కడే torque మాయమవుతుంది — maximum ninety వద్ద ఉంది.

misconception_watch.belief: Torque is maximum when p is parallel to E, because that is when the dipole is most aligned.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Alignment means τ = 0, not τ_max. τ = p × E is a cross product — it is largest when p ⊥ E (θ = 90°).

scene_composition.text: Two angles give τ = 0 — but they are not the same

θ = 0° (p ∥ E): U is LOWEST.
Nudge it → it swings back.
Stable. The dipole oscillates here.
U = −pE cos θ. Lowest at θ = 0° (stable), highest at θ = 180° (unstable). Drag θ to trace it.

tts_sentences.text_en: Torque is zero at two angles — theta zero, p along the field, and theta one eighty, p against it. But they are not equally safe.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Torque दो angles पर zero है — theta zero, जहाँ p field के साथ है, और theta one eighty, जहाँ p field के against है। लेकिन वे equally safe नहीं हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Torque రెండు angles వద్ద zero — theta zero, p field తో ఉన్నప్పుడు, మరియు theta one eighty, p field కి వ్యతిరేకంగా ఉన్నప్పుడు. కానీ అవి equally safe కాదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the dipole oscillate gently about theta zero — nudge it and it always swings back.
tts_sentences.text_hi: dipole को theta zero के आसपास gently oscillate होते देखो — इसे nudge करो और यह हमेशा वापस swing करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: dipole theta zero చుట్టూ gently oscillate అవ్వడం చూడండి — దాన్ని nudge చేయండి, అది ఎప్పుడూ తిరిగి swing అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: That is stable equilibrium — like a compass needle it always returns; on the U-meter the marker sits at the bottom, the lowest energy.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह stable equilibrium है — compass needle की तरह यह हमेशा वापस आता है; U-meter पर marker सबसे नीचे है, सबसे कम energy।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది stable equilibrium — compass needle లాగా ఇది ఎప్పుడూ తిరిగి వస్తుంది; U-meter పై marker అట్టడుగున ఉంది, అత్యల్ప energy.

scene_composition.text: θ = 180°: balanced on a knife-edge — watch it fall

θ = 180° (p against E): U is HIGHEST.
Any nudge grows → it flips around.
Unstable. Look at the U-meter on the left.

The nudge grows:
179° → 170° → 150° → races
through 90° → overshoots →
settles at θ = 0°.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now park the dipole at theta one eighty — p exactly against the field. The torque is zero again, and the U-meter sits at its very top.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब dipole को theta one eighty पर park करो — p बिल्कुल field के against। torque फिर से zero है, और U-meter बिल्कुल top पर है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు dipole ని theta one eighty వద్ద park చేయండి — p సరిగ్గా field కి వ్యతిరేకంగా. torque మళ్ళీ zero, U-meter పూర్తిగా top లో ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch closely — the tiniest nudge starts a slow creep, one seventy, one sixty, then the torque builds and the dipole races through ninety.
tts_sentences.text_hi: ध्यान से देखो — सबसे छोटा nudge एक धीमी creep शुरू करता है, one seventy, one sixty, फिर torque बढ़ता है और dipole ninety से तेज़ी से निकल जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: జాగ్రత్తగా చూడండి — అతి చిన్న nudge ఒక నెమ్మది creep మొదలుపెడుతుంది, one seventy, one sixty, తర్వాత torque పెరిగి dipole ninety గుండా వేగంగా దూసుకుపోతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: It overshoots, swings back, and settles at theta zero. An energy maximum cannot hold — unstable equilibrium always falls to stable.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह overshoot करता है, वापस swing करता है, और theta zero पर settle हो जाता है। एक energy maximum टिक नहीं सकता — unstable equilibrium हमेशा stable में गिरता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది overshoot అయ్యి, తిరిగి swing అయ్యి, theta zero వద్ద settle అవుతుంది. ఒక energy maximum నిలవదు — unstable equilibrium ఎప్పుడూ stable లోకి పడిపోతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: θ = 180° is just as stable as θ = 0°, because the torque is zero at both angles.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Only θ = 0° is stable — it is the energy minimum. θ = 180° is the energy maximum, so any nudge grows and the dipole flips to θ = 0°.

STATE_8.title: Free play — release θ and watch real physics

scene_composition.text: Free play — drag the three sliders

τ = pE sin θ
U = −pE cos θ

p doubles → τ doubles
E doubles → τ doubles
θ moves → τ traces a sine

Try these:
1. Release θ from 180° — the flip.
2. Release from 90° — the swing home.
3. Double p or E — a faster swing.

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn. Drag theta anywhere and let go — the dipole obeys tau equals p E sine theta and swings home to theta zero on its own.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब तुम्हारी बारी। theta को कहीं भी drag करो और छोड़ दो — dipole, tau equals p E sine theta को मानता है और अपने आप theta zero पर वापस swing करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీ వంతు. theta ని ఎక్కడికైనా drag చేసి వదిలేయండి — dipole, tau equals p E sine theta ని పాటిస్తూ, తనంతట తానే theta zero కి తిరిగి swing అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Release from one eighty and watch it linger, then flip. Raise p or E and the swing home gets faster.
tts_sentences.text_hi: one eighty से release करो और देखो यह पहले रुकता है, फिर flip हो जाता है। p या E बढ़ाओ और वापसी की swing तेज़ हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: one eighty నుండి release చేసి చూడండి — అది ముందు ఆగి, తర్వాత flip అవుతుంది. p లేదా E పెంచండి, తిరిగి వచ్చే swing వేగం పెరుగుతుంది.

STATE_1.label: A dipole and its moment p — no field yet
STATE_1.caption: Two charges +q and −q with the dipole moment p pointing −q → +q. The external field is not switched on yet.

STATE_2.label: Place it in a uniform field E (θ ≈ 60°)
STATE_2.caption: The uniform field E (identical blue arrows) is switched on. The dipole sits tilted at θ to the field; the yellow arc marks the angle. Drag θ to retilt it.

STATE_3.label: Each charge feels qE — the couple appears
STATE_3.caption: Each charge feels qE: +qE along E on the positive charge, −qE against E on the negative — equal, opposite, offset lines of action: a couple. Drag θ to re-tilt it.

STATE_4.label: ΣF = 0, τ ≠ 0 — the dipole rotates toward E
STATE_4.caption: The two forces sum to zero (ΣF = 0 badge), yet the couple makes a torque τ — the dipole rotates toward E without sliding anywhere. Drag θ to feel the torque rise and fall.

STATE_5.label: θ sweep 0 → 180° — τ traces a sine; max at 90°
STATE_5.caption: θ animates 0 → 180° and back on a 10-second cycle. The τ arrow scales with sin θ — zero at 0° and 180°, biggest at 90°. Grab θ to park it at the maximum.

STATE_6.label: Stable oscillation about θ = 0° + U-meter
STATE_6.caption: The dipole oscillates gently about θ = 0° (stable). The U-meter shows U = −pE cos θ: lowest at θ = 0°, highest at θ = 180°. Drag θ aside and watch it spring back.

STATE_7.label: Unstable at θ = 180° — creep, race, overshoot, settle
STATE_7.caption: The dipole starts balanced at θ ≈ 180° — the energy maximum. The tiny offset grows: a slow creep (179° → 170° → 150°), a race through 90°, an overshoot past 0°, then a damped settle into θ = 0°. The U-dot slides from the very top to the very bottom.

STATE_8.label: Free play — release θ; real τ = pE sinθ dynamics
STATE_8.caption: Sliders control p, E and θ. Let GO of θ and the dipole swings home to θ = 0° by real physics — overshoot, damped libration, settle. Release near 180° and it lingers on the knife-edge first. Raising p or E stiffens the swing.
